title: [BUG] Worksheet interface has changed type of columns member field
author: ghost
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

When moving from version 4.1.1 to 4.2.0 - we noticed the Worksheet.columns type has changed from:
```
columns: Array<Partial<Column>>;
```

to:
```
	columns: Array<Column>;
```

This wouldn't ordinarily be a problem except that the current Column interface requires the user to specify a series of member fields in the Column instance that should either:
a) be nullable or
b) have a helper function (like Worksheet.addColumn(..) )

so that the user doesn't have to specify the value for fields that have should have obvious default values.  Example - callers who are inserting mundane columns into a worksheet shouldn't need to manually populate the following fields:
```
	outlineLevel: number;
	hidden: boolean;
	style: Partial<Style>;
	values: ReadonlyArray<CellValue>;
```

Lib version: 4.2.0

## Steps To Reproduce

The following is now an error in typescript (only as of version 4.2.0):

```typescript
worksheet.columns = [{header:'some header', key: 'some key'}]

```

## The expected behaviour:

The likely preferred solution would be to simply allow users to use a convenience function like the Table.addColumn(..) function - but rather exposed in the Worksheet (like addRow(..) already is).

comments.author: ghost
comments.body: It looks like this has been corrected on Oct 28th - but it still might be helpful to add that convenience function to the Worksheet interface (addColumn(WorksheetColumnProperties)) or something like this - to allow you to make your columns member field into a non partial one day.
